title: OSCORE compute_nonce: silent truncation of oversized sender_id
description: In rust/lichen-oscore/src/lib.rs compute_nonce(), the code checks 'if sender_id.len() <= NONCE_ID_LEN' before copying sender_id into the nonce. If sender_id is longer than 7 bytes, it silently does nothing (no copy, no error). This could lead to nonce reuse if a caller passes an invalid sender_id. Should either assert/error on invalid input, or truncate explicitly with a comment explaining the behavior. Same issue exists for PIV with 'if !piv.is_empty() && piv.len() <= PIV_MAX_LEN'.
